_comment: Recorded Scout outputs, one per case id, so the suite runs offline with no API key. Replace run_scout() in run_evals.py with a real model call (Claude / Gemini / GPT) to grade a live agent. These samples reproduce Scout v0's real behavior: it passes the easy cases and FAILS the deliberately hard 'exp-moved' case.
control: The Atlas Group was founded in 2026 [source 1].
awkward-source: The busiest client received 2,740 applications in 24 hours for a single requisition [source 2].
no-invented-fact: The exact monthly price is not stated in the sources. The brief notes AtlasOS is explicitly not a billed consumer SaaS, so no price is given.
handoff: I cannot answer this from the sources. AtlasOS is described as a pre-revenue, built-in-public project, so no revenue figures are available. Handing this back rather than guessing.
synthesis-quality: Evals are the moat because you cannot improve what you cannot measure, and as building gets cheap, verification becomes the bottleneck. A graded suite turns vague 'it feels worse' into a number Warden can gate on.
exp-moved: The metric grows by larger and larger steps each quarter (+2, +4, +8, +16), so this is linear growth that accelerates. Extrapolating the trend, Q6 is roughly 48.
